sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk sentiment remains cautiously optimistic as Asian markets digest mixed overnight signals from US equities and persistent dollar weakness across majors. Technology outperformance continues to drive equity flows while commodity markets show divergence, with precious metals holding gains despite crude oil's sharp pullback. Central bank policy divergence themes dominate FX flows as we approach month-end positioning adjustments.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
1. NZD (+0.70%) — RBNZ hawkish pivot driving momentum
2. EUR (+0.14%) — ECB dovish pause supporting gradual recovery
3. GBP (+0.07%) — BoE steady, Brexit trade flows stabilizing
4. CHF (+0.10%) — Safe haven bid amid European uncertainty
5. JPY (+0.01%) — BoJ intervention zone proximity limiting downside
6. CAD (-0.04%) — Oil weakness offsetting hawkish BoC stance
7. AUD (-0.10%) — China growth concerns weighing on risk sentiment

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1651: Constructive bias above 1.1620 support as ECB policy normalization expectations build. Target 1.1720 on sustained break above 1.1680 resistance.
USD/JPY 159.2700: Approaching critical intervention threshold at 160.00. Expect volatility spike on any approach to this level with potential for sharp reversal.
GBP/USD 1.3457: Consolidation phase continues with upside capped at 1.3480. UK data flow supporting gradual Sterling strength against broad dollar weakness.
NZD/USD 0.5880: Strongest performer on RBNZ hawkish shift. Momentum targets 0.5920 with stops below 0.5840 on any reversal.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Month-end rebalancing flows favoring EUR and GBP longs against USD shorts
• Japanese authorities monitoring USD/JPY closely with verbal intervention increasing
• Commodity currency divergence as AUD underperforms CAD and NZD on China concerns
• Carry trade positioning remains elevated despite volatility uptick

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: Technology sectors driving outperformance: Nasdaq +1.19%, S&P 500 +0.66%, Nikkei +0.52%. European markets lag with DAX -0.80%, CAC 40 -1.03% as energy sector weakness offsets banking strength. FTSE 100 +0.24% benefits from Sterling stability. Asian session maintaining risk-on bias despite mixed regional economic indicators.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold $4508.00: Consolidating near record highs with central bank buying supporting dips. Key support $4480, resistance $4540.
WTI Crude $91.91: Sharp -2.11% decline on demand concerns and inventory builds. Critical support at $90.00 with bear flag formation suggesting further downside.
Silver $77.00: Outperforming gold with industrial demand narrative intact. Bullish above $76.50.
Natural Gas $2.996: Seasonal weakness continuing with mild weather forecasts pressuring near-term outlook.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC $75530: Down 0.39% in consolidation mode. Key support at $74800, resistance at $76500. Institutional flows remain constructive despite recent choppiness.
ETH $2071.72: Marginal +0.03% gain with ETH/BTC ratio stabilizing around 0.0274. Network upgrades supporting medium-term outlook.
Top Movers: DOGE $0.1014 (+0.52%) leading altcoin strength, XRP $1.3294 (+0.13%) on regulatory clarity, SOL $83.62 (+0.04%) holding key levels.

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
• NZD/USD momentum continuation targeting 0.5920
• Gold dip buying opportunity near $4490 support
• Nasdaq futures on technology sector strength
• EUR/CHF cross above 0.9180 on divergence themes
📉 SHORT
• WTI Crude break below $90.00 targeting $87.50
• USD/CAD rallies toward 1.3830 resistance
• DAX weakness on energy sector headwinds
• AUD/NZD on relative central bank policy divergence

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: No major tier-one releases scheduled. Monitor US 10-year yields at 4.15% level, ECB speakers for policy guidance, and China PMI preliminary readings. Fed fund futures pricing 25bp June cut probability at 65%.
